metadata.episode: EP012 — PTQ vs QAT: does training-aware 4-bit beat naive 4-bit? (the @skmineforever payoff)
metadata.provenance: MEASURED 2026-06-27 on GCP L4 (24 GB VRAM). Gemma 12B, two 4-bit builds (PTQ-Q4 vs QAT-Q4), same 15-task graded exam, temp 0, deterministic graders (run_quality.py) — no LLM-judge. Both builds run on the SAME box so hardware is held constant; the only variable is how the 4-bit was made.
metadata.rig: GCP L4 (24 GB VRAM); both builds on the same box.
metadata.model: Gemma 3 12B — ollama tags: gemma3:12b (PTQ-Q4, naive) vs gemma3:12b-it-qat (QAT-Q4, training-aware)
metadata.exam: same 15-task graded exam as EP004/EP011 (temp 0, deterministic graders, no LLM-judge)
metadata.credit: viewer @skmineforever (suggested QAT in EP011 comments; on-air promise paid off)
metadata.the_one_variable: how the 4-bit was made: PTQ (round a finished model down) vs QAT (train it to live at 4-bit). Same model, ~same footprint, same exam.

builds.name: PTQ-Q4
builds.kind: post-training quantization (naive 4-bit, the everyday tool / what EP011 ran)

builds.name: QAT-Q4
builds.kind: quantization-aware training (smart 4-bit, only where the maker ships it — Gemma)

_by_bucket_note: This episode's published claim is OVERALL accuracy (the video shows the 80%/80% tie + speed/size, no per-bucket chart), so the per-bucket split is recorded as null here rather than guessed. run_quality.py emits a full by_bucket on every run — re-run the two ollama tags above to regenerate it for either build.

derived.verdict: Accuracy is a flat tie (0 pts at the same 4-bit class). QAT-Q4 is ~0.8 GB larger on disk but runs ~9% faster (32 vs 29 tok/s). Net: grab QAT for the free speed where the maker ships it; PTQ-Q4 stays the everyday tool everywhere else.

modeled_labelled.cost_per_acceptable_answer: ~8% cheaper for QAT — MODELED from +9% speed at equal accuracy (less machine-time per correct answer); say 'modeled' on camera.
modeled_labelled.note: at ~the same size and equal accuracy, the faster build is cheaper per CORRECT answer.

honest_caveats: QAT only exists where the model maker ships an official build (Google does for Gemma). PTQ-Q4 stays the everyday tool for everything else.
Same model size at both builds (12B PTQ-Q4 vs 12B QAT-Q4). Comparing across sizes would confound the variable.
QAT-Q4 is slightly LARGER on disk (8.9 vs 8.1 GB), not identical — the win is speed at equal accuracy, not size.
Both builds loaded and completed all 15 tasks (0 timeouts); nothing fabricated.
